clusters.root_cause_summary: Stair centroid lies in a navmesh-disconnected component confirmed by persistent Reach_stair_centroid=False. look_for_downstair exits naturally in 2–12 steps and is never the stall site; the actual prolonged stall is in get_close_to_stair (75+ steps), which no tested intervention has gated with an exit condition.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: navmesh-disconnected stair centroid (multiple centroids tested, all False); look_for_downstair exits naturally before any budget or counter threshold fires; get_close_to_stair is the sole prolonged stall mode; all candidates share behavioral fingerprint — every intervention was a no-op. Different: look_for_downstair natural-exit duration (12 steps vs 2 steps); onset step of get_close_to_stair stall (~179 vs 164); total episode length (381 vs 239 steps).
clusters.transfer_notes: candidate_5 uniquely extended look_for_downstair to 45 steps in q3zU7Yy5E5s (vs 12 in all others), the only fingerprint deviation across both scenes — confirming that look_for_downstair duration can be stretched but the underlying navmesh disconnect is unaffected and get_close_to_stair stall always follows. This teaches that any fix restricted to look_for_downstair transfers zero improvement to this cluster; the exit condition must be attached to get_close_to_stair itself. The false-feasible pathfinder.find_path() snap confirmed in candidate_8 applies identically to both scenes, ruling out find_path()-based feasibility gates cluster-wide.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: step_budget_or_reach_centroid_false_count_exit_on_get_close_to_stair: add an exit condition inside get_close_to_stair that triggers stair disable and floor reinit when either (a) N consecutive Reach_stair_centroid=False readings accumulate or (b) a per-entry step budget T is exceeded. Crucially, use habitat pathfinder.island_radius() at the snap-point of the stair centroid to verify disconnection rather than find_path(), which returns false-feasible by snapping to nearby navigable nodes.

clusters.root_cause_summary: candidate_0 solved this episode via passive climb_stair (steps 91–102); the candidate_2 hysteresis patch diverted the agent into a get_close_to_stair→look_for_downstair oscillation that exhausted 499 steps. The toilet is 2D-reachable (feasible tag), making this a patch-induced regression rather than any structural navmesh or floor-confusion failure.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. Unique in that the episode is already solved by the baseline (candidate_0 SUCCESS), making it the only cluster where the correct intervention is reverting a prior modification rather than adding new logic. The oscillation arises because the hysteresis patch forces the agent through look_for_downstair before allowing climb, overriding the passive climb_stair detection that fires during explore mode.
clusters.transfer_notes: This scene establishes a cross-cluster guard: any patch touching the stair-approach FSM that intercepts explore→climb_stair transitions must preserve the passive climb_stair pathway. The candidate_2 regression shows that floor-hysteresis modifications cause oscillatory re-entry loops (reinits 3→6) specifically when the passive path would have succeeded — a risk that any future get_close_to_stair or look_for_downstair modification must audit before deployment.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: revert_hysteresis_patch_and_preserve_passive_climb_stair_detection: remove the candidate_2 hysteresis modification entirely, or gate it to not intercept the explore→climb_stair transition, restoring the candidate_0 behavioral fingerprint (initialize→explore→climb_stair→explore→…→SUCCESS) and the proven stair traversal at steps 91–102.

clusters.root_cause_summary: Stair traversal succeeds at step 198 (floor_step resets to 0), but floor 2 yields only 2 frontiers near the stair landing, both exhausted within ~16 floor_steps; the target room lies in a navmesh-disconnected component of floor 2 unreachable from the stair landing, so no amount of post-climb exploration or NOQUIT rescues can locate it.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. Mechanistically distinct from get_close_to_stair_navmesh_disconnect: disconnection is on the destination floor's exploration graph after a successful climb, not on the stair approach centroid before climbing. All 8 candidates share an identical behavioral fingerprint, confirming every tested lever operates either before the stair climb (causally irrelevant) or post-climb in a region that cannot generate frontiers in the disconnected component.
clusters.transfer_notes: candidate_8's pre-entry pathfinder feasibility gate confirmed that look_for_downstair is outside this scene's causal path. T4_NOQUIT rescues at steps 228 and 241 both encounter an empty frontier pool, establishing that the frontier pool must be seeded from a wider search radius or different entry point — re-triggering navigation to the same stair landing is structurally insufficient. The lesson transfers to any two-floor scene: post-climb frontier exhaustion within a small island near the landing is a distinct failure mode from pre-climb navmesh stalls.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: navmesh_subgraph_connectivity_check_before_floor2_exploration: after climb success, use habitat pathfinder.island_radius() on each frontier in the floor-2 pool to detect confinement to a small landing island; if island_radius < threshold, seed additional candidate viewpoints by sampling navigable points at geodesic distance bands (2m, 4m, 6m) from the stair landing, or attempt a fallback entry via a different stair if present, rather than exhausting the same disconnected island.

forbidden_moves.reason: Globally mandatory forbidden move across all scenes per problem specification.
Globally mandatory forbidden move across all scenes per problem specification.
look_for_downstair exits naturally in 2–12 steps in both scenes before any budget threshold can fire; applying a budget to this mode is a structural no-op for the cluster.
Same reason as step_budget_on_look_for_downstair: mode exits naturally before counter accumulates to any useful threshold (confirmed by candidate_6 in both scenes).

Gate silently falls through try/except fallback or pathfinder.find_path() snaps centroid to a nearby navigable node and returns false-feasible; confirmed zero behavioral effect in candidate_8 for both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk.
Disabling stairs from within look_for_downstair is a no-op since that mode exits naturally before the disable fires; stair re-enable still occurs when get_close_to_stair re-activates.
Navmesh disconnection is a scene geometry constraint; no DP parameter modifies navmesh topology or island connectivity.
Post-climb floor-2 navmesh disconnection is a scene geometry constraint; DP parameters control scoring and timing, not frontier reachability in a disconnected graph component.
Ruled out for both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk; additionally causes regression in mL8ThkuaVTM, making it harmful cluster-wide.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; the failure is post-climb disconnection, entirely orthogonal to stair-approach FSM hysteresis.
mL8ThkuaVTM target is 2D-reachable (feasible tag); floor confusion was explicitly ruled out — the failure is a patch-induced FSM oscillation, not multi-floor confusion.
Ruled out for mL8ThkuaVTM; the regression stems from the hysteresis patch diverting the passive climb_stair path, not from floor-switch rate — increasing DP12 does not restore the passive pathway.
